name: Comonad

x.formalRole: The categorical dual of a monad: a context you can always extract a value from and extend computations over.
x.compositionRule: extract : W a → a; extend : (W a → b) → W a → W b (dually, duplicate : W a → W (W a)).
laws: right identity: extract ∘ extend f = f
x.encoding: A type W with extract + extend/duplicate. Examples: non-empty lists, zippers, streams, the Store/Env comonads.
x.useCases: Context-dependent computations: cellular automata, image convolution, spreadsheet recalculation, focused traversals.
misuseRisks: Reaching for comonads where a simple fold suffices

def: The dual of a monad: instead of injecting values into a context (return) and sequencing (bind), a comonad lets you extract a focal value and extend a context-aware computation everywhere.
problem: Compose computations that depend on a surrounding context (neighbourhoods, history, focus) uniformly.
mechanism: Provides extract (W a → a) and extend ((W a → b) → W a → W b); duplicate exposes every sub-context, so a local function is lifted to run at every position with full context.
failures: Using a comonad where a plain map/fold is clearer
whenNot: No notion of "context around a value" in the problem
Team unfamiliar and a fold would do
myths: "Comonads are just backwards monads you’ll never use" — zippers and streaming convolutions use them naturally
